signals: AI generating test cases and test scripts
Manual exploratory testing perceived as less scalable
Leadership asking whether QA headcount can be reduced

workflow_change.before: Spent large amounts of time writing manual test cases, regression plans, and repetitive validation flows.
workflow_change.after: Uses AI to draft tests and edge cases, with more effort going into test strategy, risk framing, and validation of what should be tested.

actions: Shifted focus toward QA strategy and product risk thinking
Worked more closely with developers on prevention rather than only detection

outcome.early_effect: Low-level testing work feels more threatened, while higher-level quality judgment feels more important.

narrative.summary: AI appears to reduce the scarcity of repetitive QA execution, but not the need for judgment about product risk and testing priorities.
